OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATTINGLY, NANCY MATTINGLY, MCCABE

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS ASSERT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AGAINST DEFENDANTS. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT STATE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH "MALICE, OPPRESSION, OR FRAUD." ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS J. FEENEY, Tel No. (510) 267-7273 appearing for the Defendants;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ESTATE OF CLARITA LIMJOCO, FREDDIE LIMJOCO, AND THU LE LIMJOCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE COMPLAINT is Granted without leave to amend. Plaintiffs assert causes of action for negligence against Defendants. The complaint does not state facts establishing that Defendants acted with "malice, oppression, or fraud." ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
